key_outcome: RUN29 stayed source/test-only and added a bounded visible-proxy wrapper and association-reference signature seam for radio-like controls, threaded those safe cue buckets into the guarded fallback matcher, and removed the visible-input gate that previously prevented zero-size radio inputs from emitting bounded radio signatures. Focused guarded tests and the full unit suite both passed.
blockers: RUN29 did not spend another signer URL, so the live three-radio DocuSign cluster still needs one authorized capture-only rerun to confirm whether the new proxy/reference seam exposes a unique safe physical/business cue on the signer surface.

Even with the new bounded proxy/reference seam, guarded selection remains fail-closed until exactly one live candidate exposes an explicit physical/business physical cue without mailing/legal/virtual ambiguity or multiple matches.
uncertainty: The live controls may still expose differentiators on proxy/reference surfaces outside the current allowed tag and reference set, although RUN29 now covers zero-size inputs, visible proxy wrappers, label-for relationships, aria-labelledby, aria-describedby, aria-controls, data-tab/data-field/data-recipient-like references, and DocuSign-like reference targets.

A screenshot is optional and only useful if a future live rerun still yields empty or ambiguous proxy signatures; if needed, capture only the physical-address toggle block before any clicks, including the visible proxy controls and their immediate wrappers.
next_recommendation: If the user authorizes another signer URL, spend exactly one RUN30 live capture-only rerun with npm run bootstrap:capture:physical-address to inspect proxyReferenceSignature and zero-size input visibility for the same three radio candidates before making more source changes.
